varieties.name: Fuyu
varieties.traits: Round, squat, tomato-shaped fruit, 5-7cm diameter; bright orange-red skin and flesh; medium-sized tree to 15ft
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crisp, sweet, firm texture even when ripe; seedless; can be eaten like an apple or allowed to soften
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy, consistent producer; begins fruiting 2-3 years

varieties.name: Jiro (Ichikikei Jiro)
varieties.traits: Large, square-shaped fruit; flat with four distinct lobes; glossy orange skin; compact tree to 12-15ft
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, crisp, sweet with mild flavor; thin, tender skin; seedless; larger than Fuyu
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent yields when mature; can be erratic when young; very productive
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Grown commercially in Queensland. Thrives in subtropical climates. More cold-hardy than Fuyu. Heat and humidity resistant. Self-pollinating. Ripens late October-November. Fruits early March in subtropics. Popular with humid subtropical climates orchardists.
varieties.availability: Perrys Fruit Nursery, Daley's Fruit, Rossmount Orchard, Stark Bros

varieties.name: Izu
varieties.traits: Small to medium-sized fruit, tomato-shaped; bright orange; semi-dwarf tree, compact growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, crisp when firm; pale orange flesh; seedless; excellent quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good cropping; productive for smaller tree size
varieties.growing_notes: VERY GOOD FOR humid subtropical climates - Early ripening (September-October). More cold-hardy than Fuyu but adapts well to Humid subtropical mild winters. Suitable for smaller humid subtropical climates backyards. Heat tolerant. Ideal for subtropical Queensland.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit, Garden Express, Nurseries Online Australia

varieties.name: Dai Dai Maru
varieties.traits: Medium to large, distinctive square shape similar to Fuyu; attractive tree with weeping form
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, good quality; firm texture; non-astringent
varieties.growing_notes: GOOD FOR humid subtropical climates - Popular in Australian home gardens. Ornamental value with weeping form. Most often seen in Queensland markets. Adapts to subtropical conditions. Heat tolerant.

varieties.name: Jiro Dwarf
varieties.traits: Compact tree reaching only 2m height; large, flat fruit; perfect for containers and small spaces
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent fruit quality; sweet, crisp; seedless
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields despite smaller tree size

varieties.name: Fuyu Dwarf
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf tree to 2m with pruning; same fruit as standard Fuyu; manageable size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Identical to Fuyu - sweet, crisp, seedless; large, flat, orange-red fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent yields; heavy bearer despite smaller tree
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT FOR humid subtropical climates BACKYARDS - Best for growing in pots or small humid subtropical climates yards. Widely available in Australian nurseries. Heat tolerant. Low maintenance. Self-pollinating. Ripens October-November.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit, Garden Express, Nurseries Online Australia

varieties.name: Suruga
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit; reddish-orange skin; attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet - one of the sweetest non-astringent types; spicy-sweet flavor

varieties.name: Gosho (Giant Fuyu)
varieties.traits: Very large, heavy fruit - bigger version of Fuyu; similar square shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Tannin-free; sweet with date/apricot flavor; excellent quality
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Adapts to subtropical conditions. Heat tolerant. Impressive fruit size for show and markets.

varieties.name: Maekawa Jiro
varieties.traits: Large to extra-large fruit; compact tree under 15ft; prone to tip cracking in extreme heat
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sugar cane-like sweetness; lacks spicy notes; excellent quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; tree not very vigorous but productive
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Adapts to subtropical climate. Can crack in extreme heat - harvest promptly when ripe. Ripens late October-November.

varieties.name: Imoto
varieties.traits: Squat Fuyu/Jiro shape; reddish-brown skin when ripe rather than orange
varieties.flesh_fruit: Extremely sweet; firm flesh; no astringency

varieties.name: Matsumoto-Wase Fuyu
varieties.traits: Vigorous tree 16-18ft; larger than original Fuyu
varieties.flesh_fruit: Nearly identical flavor to Fuyu - difficult to distinguish; sweet and crisp
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Ripens late September-October. Earlier than standard Fuyu. Heat and humidity tolerant. Vigorous grower suited to subtropical Queensland.

varieties.name: Hanafuyu
varieties.traits: Dwarf tree, only 10ft at maturity; oblong fruit shape; very large fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet whether hard or soft; versatile eating stages
varieties.yield_notes: Biennial bearing tendency - heavy crop one year, lighter the next
varieties.growing_notes: GOOD FOR humid subtropical climates - Dwarf size perfect for small humid subtropical climates yards. Biennial bearing pattern. Heat tolerant.

varieties.name: Nightingale
varieties.traits: Large fruit tending to red color; early ripening; small tree suitable for netting
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet when fully ripe and soft; jelly-like consistency; excellent quality
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific yields - excellent producer; lots of large fruit
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Recommended by Australian nurseries. Early ripening suits humid subtropical climates climate. Grows well in subtropical Queensland. Popular in home gardens. Birds love it - use netting. Heat tolerant.

varieties.name: Hachiya
varieties.traits: Large, acorn-shaped fruit; bright orange skin; vigorous, upright tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Intensely sweet when ripe - honeyed with tropical fruit notes; jelly-like, custardy; seedless
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive; heavy bearer; consistent crops

varieties.name: Flat Seedless
varieties.traits: Large, flat fruit; vigorous tree; pale fruit color
varieties.flesh_fruit: Seedless; pale flesh; sweet when fully soft

varieties.name: Hyakume (Chocolate/Cinnamon Persimmon)
varieties.traits: Large, round fruit; bright orange skin; brown-flecked flesh when pollinated
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light cinnamon color; brown sugar taste; spicy-sweet; seeded varieties are sweetest

varieties.name: Maru (Chocolate Persimmon, Tsurunoko)
varieties.traits: Small to medium oblong fruit; bright orange skin; dark brown interior
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep brown jelly-like flesh; very sweet but not overly sweet; hints of nutmeg; decadent texture
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Revered heirloom grown by artisanal orchardists. Unique chocolate appearance (not flavor). Connoisseur's choice. Heat tolerant.

varieties.name: Saijo
varieties.origin: Japan, name means 'the very best one'
varieties.traits: Small to medium oblong, conical fruit; 1.5-2in long; bright orange; beautiful tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Extremely sweet - honey-like; hints of mango/papaya; seedless; translucent when ripe

varieties.name: Tanenashi
varieties.traits: Medium-large conical fruit; light orange to red-orange skin; vigorous, upright tree to 30ft
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow-orange flesh; dry texture when ripe; sweet, rich flavor; completely seedless
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy, prolific producer; stores extremely well on tree

varieties.name: Tamopan
varieties.traits: Very large, flat fruit with distinctive ring constriction around middle; tomato with a hat shape; large tree to 30ft
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet when fully soft; orange flesh; unique appearance

varieties.name: Eureka
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, flat-shaped fruit; red skin; small, relatively compact tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Extremely high quality; typically contains seeds; excellent texture that stores well
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producing; self-fruitful; proven best commercial variety in some regions
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Popular across US and Asia. Heat zones 8-11. Exceptional fruit quality. Ripens late October-early November. Stores well.

varieties.name: Great Wall
varieties.origin: China, discovered near Great Wall by J. Russel Smith in 1920s
varieties.traits: Medium orange fruit; erect growing tree, taller than Fuyu; compact, stays under 20ft
varieties.flesh_fruit: Cinnamon-orange flesh when ripe; sweet

varieties.name: Sheng
varieties.traits: Flat shape resembling heirloom tomato; lobed sections around calyx
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with notes of clove and cinnamon; unique flavor profile
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Chinese heirloom. Zones 6-9 so more cold-hardy but adapts to humid subtropical climates. Unique spiced flavor.

varieties.name: Rojo Brillante (Sharon Fruit)
varieties.traits: Large, deep orange-red fruit; commercially important in Spain
varieties.flesh_fruit: Astringent but commercially treated with CO2 to remove astringency while still firm; very sweet; seedless
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Heat tolerant. Major marketing success in Europe. Can be grown successfully in humid subtropical climates.

varieties.name: Pomelo
varieties.traits: Prolific bearer; large to extra-large fruit; deep orange
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet when fully ripe; luscious jelly-like consistency
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Heat tolerant. Extremely productive for home gardens.

varieties.name: Triumph (Tropical Persimmon, Sharon Fruit) 🌱
varieties.origin: Developed for warm climates; grown in Florida, Israel; name varies by region
varieties.traits: Small 6-7cm diameter; oblate shape; tomato-like appearance; thin, smooth skin; highly productive
varieties.flesh_fruit: Astringent when firm, but can be CO2-treated; dark orange-red when ripe; very soft, jelly-like when ready; seedless
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy, reliable crops in tropical conditions

varieties.name: Fuyu
varieties.traits: Round, squat fruit 5-7cm; bright orange; semi-dwarf to 15ft; heat-tolerant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Non-astringent; crisp and sweet when firm; seedless; excellent fresh eating
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Native to subtropical climates, tolerates heat and humidity very well. Requires minimal chill (100-200 hours). Can adapt to tropical conditions with proper care. Most widely grown persimmon globally. Heat zones 7-11 indicates good heat tolerance. Handles monsoon humidity. Self-fertile.
varieties.availability: M-Tech Gardens India, ZXXYANBI India (grafted plants), tropical monsoon climates nurseries, Amazon India

varieties.name: Hachiya
varieties.traits: Large acorn-shaped fruit; bright orange; vigorous tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Astringent until soft; intensely sweet, honeyed; jelly-like; seedless
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Requires hot summers to ripen fruit fully - tropical monsoon climates provides this. Best flavor and quality in warm climates. Heat zones 7-10. Self-fertile. Handles high heat well. May struggle with excessive monsoon moisture without proper drainage.
varieties.availability: Indian nurseries, Rudra Agro Nursery Pune, specialty tropical fruit suppliers

varieties.name: Tanenashi
varieties.traits: Medium-large conical fruit; orange-red; vigorous tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow-orange; sweet, rich; completely seedless; dry texture

varieties.name: Eureka
varieties.traits: Medium, flat fruit; red skin; compact tree; self-fruitful
varieties.flesh_fruit: Extremely high quality; contains seeds; stores well
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Popular across Asia. Heat zones 8-11 indicating excellent heat tolerance. Can adapt to tropical conditions. Self-fertile advantage.

varieties.name: Jiro (Ichikikei Jiro)
varieties.traits: Large, square fruit; flat with lobes; compact tree 12-15ft
varieties.flesh_fruit: Non-astringent; firm, crisp, sweet; seedless; thin tender skin
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Humidity tolerant. More cold-hardy than Fuyu but also heat-adaptable. Self-pollinating. Can handle tropical conditions with good drainage.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries in India, international suppliers

varieties.name: Izu
varieties.traits: Small to medium, tomato-shaped; bright orange; semi-dwarf
varieties.flesh_fruit: Non-astringent; sweet, crisp; pale orange flesh; seedless
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Early ripening suits tropical climate. Heat tolerant. More cold-hardy than Fuyu, indicating climate adaptability. Compact size good for tropical monsoon climates home gardens.

varieties.name: Suruga
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit; reddish-orange skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet - one of sweetest non-astringents; spicy-sweet; no astringency
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Researched for subtropical Australia (Queensland) indicating good heat/humidity tolerance. Heat zones 7-11. Can adapt to tropical conditions.

varieties.name: Ormond
varieties.traits: 2.5in long, 2in diameter; reddish-yellow outside, deep orange-red inside; fruits hang in clusters like grapes; vigorous tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, moderately juicy; astringent until soft; large seeds
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR tropical monsoon climates - SPECIFICALLY SUITED TO WARMER CLIMATES. Developed in Florida (similar tropical climate to tropical monsoon climates). Breaks bud early in spring, increasing chances of fruit set in tropical areas. Mid-sized tree suited to tropical monsoon climates conditions. Heat and humidity adapted.

varieties.name: Coffee Cake
varieties.traits: Brownish flesh; firm when ripe; medium-sized fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, rich, spicy - named after flavor; non-astringent; unique taste
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; needs pollinator (Chocolate variety best)
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Heat zones 7-10. Ripens a month before Fuyu (fall/winter ripening). Warm climate adapted. Unique spiced flavor profile.

varieties.name: Chocolate (American Persimmon hybrid)
varieties.traits: Small to medium oblong fruit; bright red skin; chocolate-colored flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, dotted chocolate specks; sweet with cocoa hints; non-astringent (rare for American type)
varieties.yield_notes: Good production; self-fruitful (rare for American persimmons)
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Heat zones 7-10. Heat tolerant. Self-fertile advantage. Unique chocolate flesh. Ripens late Oct-November.

varieties.name: Diospyros kaki (Common Persimmon - Seedling Type) 🌱
varieties.origin: Various regions - seedling selections; traditional types
varieties.traits: Variable traits; grown from seed; ornamental tree with orange autumn foliage
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality varies; generally astringent; fruits in 5-7 years from seed
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Seedling persimmons often more adaptable to varied climates. Used as rootstock. Can produce in tropical/subtropical conditions. Available in tropical monsoon climates nurseries. More genetic diversity for climate adaptation. Good for tropical monsoon climates experimentation.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Garden, Zoropic Enterprise, M-Tech Gardens, local tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Japani Phal (Local Indian Name)
varieties.origin: Traditional name in India for persimmon; multiple varieties sold under this name
varieties.traits: Variable - general term for persimmons in North India (Himachal, J&K, Uttarakhand)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange-red color; tomato-like appearance; soft, sweet when fully ripe
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS NAME IN INDIA - Cultivated in Indian temperate regions (Himachal, J&K, Tamil Nadu, Uttarakhand) but some selections may adapt to tropical monsoon climates with proper care. Look for heat-tolerant selections. Traditional Indian cultivation knowledge. Also known as Amarphal (Hindi).
varieties.availability: Indian nurseries nationwide, Rudra Agro Nursery Pune, regional nurseries

varieties.name: Nikita's Gift
varieties.origin: Ukraine/Russia, hybrid: Rosseyanka (D. kaki x D. virginiana) x D. kaki
varieties.traits: 2.5-3in fruit; flat bottom; dark reddish-orange; small to medium tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptionally sweet when ripe; astringent; loses astringency easier than many varieties
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Very cold-hardy but ALSO heat-adaptable due to hybrid vigor. Ripens earlier than Saijo. Better for areas with long growing season but lacking extreme heat. Good for Tropical monsoon moderate heat with humidity.

varieties.name: Rosseyanka
varieties.origin: Ukraine, hybrid: D. kaki x D. virginiana
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit; American persimmon flavor profile
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet; resembles American persimmon; astringent until soft
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates - Very cold-hardy to -25°F but hybrid vigor gives climate adaptability. Can handle temperature extremes. Good for experimental tropical monsoon climates cultivation.
